fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to new hampshire, pharmacy technician compact new hampshire, new hampshire pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)
board.mailing_address:
NH Office of Professional Licensure and Certification
7 Eagle Square
Concord, NH 03301

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in New Hampshire.

steps.title: 1. Create Account and Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the OPLC Online Licensing Portal (nh.lincware.com/Applicant/Login.aspx), create a new account, and then log in. Select the 'New Applications' tab and choose 'Pharmacy Technician' to start your application.

steps.title: 2. Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form accurately. Review your information and pay the non-refundable application fee of $20.00 through the portal.

steps.title: 3. Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or ensure submission of the following documents as instructed by the portal or the Pharmacy Technician Application Checklist: Proof of high school diploma or GED, your National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) eProfile ID, and verification of current national certification from either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA).

steps.title: 4. Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided during the online application process or on the OPLC website's Criminal Background Check Information page (oplc.nh.gov/criminal-background-check-information) to complete the required state and federal criminal background check, which includes fingerprinting.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review
steps.description: Once all application materials, fees, and background check results are received, the New Hampshire Board of Pharmacy will review your application. You can monitor the status of your application through the OPLC Online Licensing Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in New Hampshire. To work in New Hampshire, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in New Hampshire
hero.dek: Apply online through New Hampshire's OPLC Online Licensing Portal (nh.lincware.com/Applicant/Login.aspx). Create an account, select the Pharmacy Technician application, and submit all required documentation and fees.

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account on the OPLC Online Licensing Portal by clicking 'Register A New Account'. Once logged in, navigate to 'New Applications' and select the 'Pharmacy Technician' application to begin.
